abilities.name: Leader
abilities.prose: Groups of 4+ are led by a higher level cleric (1d10: 1–4: 2nd level, 5–7: 3rd level, 8–9: 4th level, 10: 5th level). Choose or roll the leader’s spells.

monsters.intro: 1st level clerics on a quest for their deity.
monsters.name: Acolyte

abilities.name: Size
abilities.prose: 16’ tall, 4’ across / 24’ tall, 6’ across / 32’ tall, 8’ across.

abilities.name: Whirlwind
abilities.prose: Creatures with less than 2HD swept aside (save versus death).

abilities.name: Harm flying creatures
abilities.prose: Inflict extra 1d8 damage.

abilities.name: Mundane damage immunity
abilities.prose: Can only be harmed by magical attacks.

monsters.intro: Huge vortexes of whirling air.
monsters.name: Air Elemental (Greater)

abilities.name: Mundane damage immunity
abilities.prose: Can only be harmed by magical attacks.

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by gas; unaffected by charm, hold, and sleep spells.

abilities.name: Tracking
abilities.prose: Without error.

abilities.name: Detect invisible creatures
abilities.prose: Within 60’.

abilities.name: Other materials
abilities.prose: Golems formed of other materials are also possible.

abilities.name: Constructing
abilities.prose: A very complex, expensive, and time-consuming process.

monsters.intro: Constructed in the form of giant cats (e.g. lions or tigers).
monsters.name: Amber Golem

abilities.name: Territorial
abilities.prose: Defend their lair with threats and, if this is ignored, violence.

monsters.intro: Albino, herbivorous, gorilla-like apes that live in caves and emerge at night to forage.
monsters.name: Ape, White

abilities.name: Trickery
abilities.prose: Use disguise or trickery to surprise victims.

abilities.name: Leader
abilities.prose: May have a leader of 2nd level or higher (any human class).

abilities.name: Hoard
abilities.prose: Only have treasure type A when encountered in their wilderness lair.

monsters.intro: NPC thieves who live by robbery.
monsters.name: Bandit

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: Characters surprised by a basilisk meet its gaze.

abilities.name: Petrifying touch
abilities.prose: Anyone touched by a basilisk is turned to stone (save vs petrify).

abilities.name: Petrifying gaze
abilities.prose: Anyone meeting a basilisk’s gaze is turned to stone (save versus petrify). Unless averting eyes or using a mirror, characters in melee are affected each round.

abilities.name: Averting eyes
abilities.prose: -4 penalty to-hit; the basilisk gains a +2 bonus to attack.

abilities.name: Mirrors
abilities.prose: The reflection of a basilisk is harmless. Fighting by looking in a mirror incurs a -1 penalty to attack. If a basilisk sees its own reflection (2-in-6 chance), it must save or be petrified.

monsters.intro: 10’ long, serpentine lizards. Unintelligent, but highly magical. Dwell in caverns and twisted brambles.
monsters.name: Basilisk

abilities.name: Battle rage
abilities.prose: +2 to hit humans and similar humanoids (e.g. orcs, goblins). Rage sometimes makes them attack their allies.

abilities.name: Hoard
abilities.prose: Only have treasure type B when encountered in the wilderness.

monsters.intro: Fighters who enter a rage in battle. They never take prisoners.
monsters.name: Berserker

abilities.name: Bear hug
abilities.prose: If a victim is hit by both paws in the same round, the bear hugs for an extra 2d8 automatic damage.

abilities.name: Defensive
abilities.prose: Adults will protect their young with their lives, but otherwise only attack if cornered.

abilities.name: Camp raids
abilities.prose: Sometimes raid camps, for food (especially fish and sweet treats).

monsters.intro: ‎6’ tall; favour eating berries and roots.
monsters.name: Black Bear

abilities.name: Breath weapon
abilities.prose: 60’ long line of acid.

abilities.name: [Language](/srd/index.php/Languages "Languages") and spells
abilities.prose: 20%; 4 × 1st level.

abilities.name: Behaviour
abilities.prose: Chaotic dragons usually try to eat humans, but may sometimes capture them. Neutral dragons may attack or ignore humans. Lawful dragons may aid parties worthy of the honour.

abilities.name: Pride
abilities.prose: Dragons are immensely proud creatures and will always listen to flattery.

abilities.name: Attack pattern
abilities.prose: A dragon always attacks first with its breath weapon, then either breathes again or makes melee attacks (equal chance of either).

abilities.name: Energy immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by their own breath weapon or lesser versions thereof. Automatically save versus similar attack forms. (For example, a red dragon is immune to flaming oil and suffers half damage from fire ball spells.)

abilities.name: Subduing
abilities.prose: Will surrender if reduced to 0hp by non-lethal attacks (see Subduing), admitting that it has been defeated. (Subdual damage does not reduce the damage done by the breath weapon.) A subdued dragon will attempt to escape or attack its captors, if the opportunity presents itself or if given a suicidal command. A subdued dragon may be sold for up to 1,000gp per hp.

abilities.name: Age
abilities.prose: The following stats describe dragons of average size. Younger dragons may have up to 3 HD less and ¼ or ½ as much treasure. Older dragons may have up to 3 HD more and twice as much treasure.

abilities.name: Lairs
abilities.prose: A dragon’s treasure is always kept in its well-hidden lair and is seldom unguarded.

monsters.intro: Dwell in swamps and marshes.
monsters.name: Black Dragon

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Only harmed by fire-based attacks.

abilities.name: Division
abilities.prose: Non-fire attacks (including spells) cause the pudding to divide. Each hit creates a 2HD pudding that does 1d8 damage.

abilities.name: Erode wood and metal
abilities.prose: Can dissolve wood or metal in one turn.

abilities.name: Cling
abilities.prose: Can move across walls and ceilings.

abilities.name: Seep
abilities.prose: Can squeeze through small holes and cracks.

monsters.intro: Huge (5’–30’ across), mindless blobs of amorphous black jelly. Driven by ravenous hunger.
monsters.name: Black Pudding

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Causes death in 1 turn (save versus poison).

abilities.name: Webs
abilities.prose: Creatures caught in webs become entangled and unable to move. Breaking free depends on Strength: 2d4 turns for strength in the normal human range; 4 rounds for strength above 18; 2 rounds for creatures with giant strength. The webs can be destroyed by fire in two rounds. All creatures in a flaming web suffer 1d6 points of damage.

monsters.intro: 6’ long, black spiders with a red hourglass pattern on their abdomens. Dwell in web-filled lairs and sometimes prey on humans.
monsters.name: Black Widow

abilities.name: Blink
abilities.prose: In combat, teleport close to an enemy, attack, then reappear 1d4 × 10’ away. If they have initiative, can blink away without the opponent being able to counter-attack.

abilities.name: Disappear
abilities.prose: If in serious danger, the pack can flee by entirely disappearing.

abilities.name: Hate [warp beasts](/srd/index.php/Warp_Beast "Warp Beast")
abilities.prose: Always attack.

monsters.intro: Highly intelligent, dingo-like dogs that live in packs. Have the innate ability to blink in and out of existence.
monsters.name: Blink Dog

abilities.name: Breath weapon
abilities.prose: 100’ long line of lightning.

abilities.name: [Language](/srd/index.php/Languages "Languages") and spells
abilities.prose: 40%; 4 × 1st level, 4 × 2nd level.

abilities.name: Lairs
abilities.prose: A dragon’s treasure is always kept in its well-hidden lair and is seldom unguarded.

monsters.intro: Favour open plains and deserts.
monsters.name: Blue Dragon

monsters.intro: Omnivorous wild boars that dwell primarily in forests. Can be irascible and dangerous, if disturbed.
monsters.name: Boar

abilities.name: Mundane damage immunity
abilities.prose: Can only be harmed by magical attacks.

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by gas; unaffected by charm, hold, and sleep spells.

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: 4×1-handed or 2×2-handed.

abilities.name: Attack multiple opponents
abilities.prose: Up to 2 per round.

abilities.name: Energy immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by fire, cold, and electricity.

abilities.name: Other materials
abilities.prose: Golems formed of other materials are also possible.

abilities.name: Constructing
abilities.prose: A very complex, expensive, and time-consuming process.

monsters.intro: 6’ tall constructs of human bone, crafted into a humanoid shape. Have four arms, attached at different places on the torso.
monsters.name: Bone Golem

abilities.name: Footmen
abilities.prose: Half the group has: leather armour, shield, sword, shortbow.

abilities.name: Cavalry
abilities.prose: The other half is mounted on riding horses and has: chainmail, shield, sword.

abilities.name: Leaders and commanders
abilities.prose: For every 20 brigands, there is a leader (2nd level fighter). For every 40 brigands, there is a commander (4th level fighter). Leaders ride war horses (with barding) and have: plate mail, sword, lance.

abilities.name: Fortified camps
abilities.prose: Bands commonly combine and live in a camp of 5d6 × 10 brigands.

abilities.name: Camp leaders
abilities.prose: Combined camps are led by a 9th level fighter, plus a 5th level fighter for every 50 brigands. Also 50% chance of a magic-user (level 1d3 + 8); 30% chance of a cleric (8th level).

monsters.intro: Outlaws and mercenaries who make a living by raiding settlements and attacking travellers.
monsters.name: Brigand

abilities.name: Mundane damage immunity
abilities.prose: Can only be harmed by magical attacks.

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by gas; unaffected by charm, hold, and sleep spells.

abilities.name: Flaming blood
abilities.prose: If damaged by an edged weapon, emits a spurt of liquid fire: attacker suffers 2d6 damage (save versus death to avoid).

abilities.name: Fire immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by fire.

abilities.name: Other materials
abilities.prose: Golems formed of other materials are also possible.

abilities.name: Constructing
abilities.prose: A very complex, expensive, and time-consuming process.

monsters.intro: Bronze constructs that resemble fire giants, possessing a great internal heat.
monsters.name: Bronze Golem

abilities.name: Ships and crew
abilities.prose: Depends where encountered. Rivers or lakes: 1d8 river boats (1d2 × 10 buccaneers each); coastal waters: 1d6 small galleys (1d3+1 × 10 buccaneers each); any: 1d4 longships (1d3+2 × 10 buccaneers each); ocean: 1d3 small warships (1d5+3 × 10 buccaneers each). (See Water Vessels for details on ships.)

abilities.name: Arms
abilities.prose: 60% of group have: leather armour, sword; 30% have: leather armour, sword, crossbow; 10% have: chainmail, sword, crossbow.

abilities.name: Leaders and captains
abilities.prose: For every 30 buccaneers, there is a 4th level fighter. Each ship has a captain (7th level fighter).

abilities.name: Fleet commander
abilities.prose: 9th level fighter. 30% chance of a magic-user (level 1d2 + 9); 25% chance of a cleric (8th level).

abilities.name: Treasure
abilities.prose: Divided between vessels. Instead of carrying aboard, may have a map to where it is buried.

abilities.name: Havens
abilities.prose: Lawless, fortified, coastal towns may act as a haven for buccaneers and pirates.

monsters.intro: Sailors who make a living by raiding coastal settlements and robbing other ships. Typically ply rivers, lakes, and coastal waters; occasionally oceans.
monsters.name: Buccaneer

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–3, due to stealth.

monsters.intro: Large, hairy goblins with an ungainly gait. Favour attacking by surprise.
monsters.name: Bugbear

abilities.name: Blood scent
abilities.prose: Can detect blood in water up to 300’ away.

abilities.name: Feeding frenzy
abilities.prose: Triggered by the scent of blood: always attack; no morale checks.

abilities.name: Ram
abilities.prose: Stunned for 3 rounds (save versus paralysis).

monsters.intro: 8’ long; brown colouration. Attack by ramming, then biting the stunned prey.
monsters.name: Bull Shark

abilities.name: Swallow whole
abilities.prose: On a natural 19 or 20 attack roll. Inside the monster’s belly: suffer 1d8 damage per round (until the caecilia dies); may attack with a dagger at -4 to hit; body digested in 6 turns after death.

monsters.intro: Gigantic (30’ long), grey, worm-like amphibians, with huge, toothed maws.
monsters.name: Caecilia

abilities.name: Ill-tempered
abilities.prose: Bite or kick creatures in their way, including owners.

abilities.name: Water
abilities.prose: After drinking well, can survive 2 weeks without water.

abilities.name: Desert travel
abilities.prose: Move at full speed through broken lands and deserts.

abilities.name: Carry loads
abilities.prose: Up to 3,000 coins unencumbered; up to 6,000 at half speed.

abilities.name: Charge attacks
abilities.prose: Are not possible, when mounted on a camel.

monsters.intro: Irascible animals that are adapted to life in dry climates. Often used for transportation in deserts.
monsters.name: Camel

abilities.name: Paralysis
abilities.prose: A hit by a tentacle causes paralysis for 2d4 turns (save versus paralysis). Paralysed victims will be devoured, if the crawler is left in peace.

abilities.name: Cling
abilities.prose: Can walk on walls and ceilings.

monsters.intro: 9’ long, 3’ high, many-legged, segmented worms with a ring of 2’ long tentacles around their mouths.
monsters.name: Carcass Crawler

abilities.name: Bear hug
abilities.prose: If a victim is hit by both paws in the same round, the bear hugs for an extra 2d8 automatic damage.

abilities.name: Sense of smell
abilities.prose: Poor eyesight, but keen sense of smell. When hungry, will follow a track of blood by scent.

monsters.intro: Ferocious, 15’ tall grizzly bears found in caves and Lost World settings. Omnivorous, but prefer meat, including humans.
monsters.name: Cave Bear

abilities.name: Blend in with stone
abilities.prose: May be overlooked or mistaken for statues, due to their stony colouration.

abilities.name: Shriek
abilities.prose: If attacked or frightened, shriek to warn others. This may attract wandering monsters (20% chance per round).

abilities.name: Jump
abilities.prose: Very skittish. If attacked, usually flee by jumping up to 60’ then flying away. 50% chance of jumping at a random opponent, in which case treat the jump as an attack.

abilities.name: Spit
abilities.prose: Used defensively. 10’ range. Target treated as AC 9 [10]. The affected character is covered in stinking spittle: unable to act for 1 turn (save versus poison). Until the goo is washed off, others who come within 5’ must also save versus poison or be violently sick.

abilities.name: Poison immunity
abilities.prose: Immune to yellow mould and most poisons, due to their habit of eating fungi.

monsters.intro: 2–3’ long, herbivorous, giant crickets that dwell in caverns.
monsters.name: Cave Locust

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Bows, clubs, lances.

abilities.name: Lair
abilities.prose: Hidden in dense woods, along twisting, guarded paths.

abilities.name: Females and young
abilities.prose: Normally remain in the lair. Flee if attacked. Young have 2HD and make 2 hoof attacks (1d2) and 1 weapon attack (1d4 or by weapon).

monsters.intro: Fantastic creatures with the legs and body of a horse, and the upper body and head of a human. Live in small family or tribal groups, in wild meadows and isolated forests.
monsters.name: Centaur

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Causes victims to become horribly sick for ten days (save versus poison): no physical activity possible except half speed movement.

monsters.intro: 1’ long centipedes that dwell in dark, damp locations.
monsters.name: Centipede, Giant

abilities.name: Attack pattern
abilities.prose: Dragon head: 50% chance of breath attack, otherwise bites.

abilities.name: Breath weapon
abilities.prose: Cone of fire: end 10’ wide, 50’ long. Can be used up to three times per day.

abilities.name: Uses fire
abilities.prose: Monsters that use fire are unharmed by burning oil.

monsters.intro: Horrific, hybrid monstrosities with the forequarters of a lion, the hindquarters of a goat, the wings of a dragon, and three heads: goat, lion, and dragon. Dwell in wild, hilly areas, or sometimes in dungeons.
monsters.name: Chimera

abilities.name: Keen sight and smell
abilities.prose: Only surprised on a 1.

abilities.name: Boulder throwing
abilities.prose: Up to 200’.

abilities.name: Castle
abilities.prose: Built into mountain-sides or floating on cloud banks.

abilities.name: Guardians
abilities.prose: 3d6 giant hawks or (only in mountains) 6d6 dire wolves.

abilities.name: Hate intruders
abilities.prose: May block mountain passes to ensure isolation.

monsters.intro: Aggressive, 20’ tall humanoids with skin and hair ranging from grey to white. Dress in pale robes.
monsters.name: Cloud Giant

abilities.name: Petrification
abilities.prose: Anyone touched is turned to stone (save versus petrify).

monsters.intro: Small, magical, bird / reptile hybrids with long serpent tails and the head, legs, and wings of a cockerel. Live in all environments.
monsters.name: Cockatrice

abilities.name: Ravenous
abilities.prose: Attack any moving creature.

monsters.intro: Unintelligent crustaceans that lurk in coastal waters and creep along beaches. Cannot swim.
monsters.name: Crab, Giant

abilities.name: Ambush
abilities.prose: Attack by dropping on victims from above.

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–4, due to camouflage.

abilities.name: Cling
abilities.prose: Can walk on walls and ceilings.

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Causes death in 1d4 turns (save versus poison with a +2 bonus).

monsters.intro: 5’ long hunting spiders that can change their colour to match their surroundings.
monsters.name: Crab Spider

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Unaffected by sleep spells.

abilities.name: Attacks
abilities.prose: Depend on form of statue (humanoid statues may use weapons, animal statues use claws, etc.).

abilities.name: Magical origins
abilities.prose: Created by powerful wizards.

monsters.intro: Formed of crystals. Often human in form.
monsters.name: Crystal Living Statue

abilities.name: Attack penalty
abilities.prose: -2 penalty on all hit rolls, due to limited depth perception.

abilities.name: Rock throwing
abilities.prose: Up to 200’.

abilities.name: Slow-witted
abilities.prose: Can be tricked by clever PCs.

abilities.name: Curse
abilities.prose: 1-in-20 cyclops can place a curse on a character once a week. Save versus spells or be afflicted by a curse of the referee’s choosing. (Maximum possible effects: -2 penalty to saves, -4 penalty to hit, an ability score reduced by 50%.)

monsters.intro: 20’ tall humanoids with a single, central eye. Dwell in caves, alone or in small groups. Cultivate grapes and raise sheep.
monsters.name: Cyclops

abilities.name: Mounted
abilities.prose: On warhorses.

abilities.name: Arms
abilities.prose: 50% of group have: leather armour, shield, lance; 25% have: leather armour, shield, shortbow, sword; 25% have: chainmail, shield, lance.

abilities.name: Camps
abilities.prose: Groups commonly combine and live in a camp or tribe of up to 300 dervishes, plus women, children, and animals. 25% chance of a wood or brick enclosure.

abilities.name: Camp leaders
abilities.prose: Led by 10th level cleric.

abilities.name: Intolerant
abilities.prose: Of other religious views.

abilities.name: Holy war
abilities.prose: Occasionally wage war against other religious factions. Will slay people of different faith, or take them prisoner. Prisoners must convert; otherwise killed or used as slaves. Characters of Lawful alignment may be asked to join the holy war. Dervishes are highly suspicious of those who refuse (without good reason).

monsters.intro: Fanatically religious, nomadic people who wander steppes and desert regions, living in tents.
monsters.name: Dervish

abilities.name: Shapechange
abilities.prose: Only at night.

abilities.name: Ambush
abilities.prose: Prefer to attack by surprise.

abilities.name: Charm person
abilities.prose: 3 times a day. Save versus spells at -2 or be charmed: move towards the devil swine (resisting those who try to prevent it); obey the devil swine’s commands (if understood); defend the devil swine; unable to cast spells or use magic items; unable to harm the devil swine. Killing the devil swine breaks the charm.

abilities.name: Charmed victims
abilities.prose: 1d4-1 accompany a devil swine.

abilities.name: Human form
abilities.prose: Have physical characteristics reminiscent of the associated animal type.

abilities.name: Mundane damage immunity
abilities.prose: In animal form, can only harmed by silver weapons or magic.

abilities.name: Languages
abilities.prose: In human form, can speak normally. In animal form, can only speak with animals of the associated type.

abilities.name: Armour
abilities.prose: Not used because it hinders shape-changing.

abilities.name: Summon animals
abilities.prose: Can summon 1–2 animals of the associated type from the surrounding area (wererats summon giant rats). These arrive in 1d4 rounds.

abilities.name: Wolfsbane
abilities.prose: If hit, must save versus poison or flee in terror.

abilities.name: Reversion
abilities.prose: If killed, a lycanthrope reverts to its human form.

abilities.name: Scent
abilities.prose: Horses and some other animals can smell lycanthropes and will become afraid.

abilities.name: Infection
abilities.prose: A character who loses more than ½ their hit points from lycanthropes’ natural attacks (i.e. bites, claws) contracts lycanthropy. Humans become were-creatures of the same type (run by the referee, henceforth); non-humans die. The disease takes full effect in 2d12 days, showing signs of infection after half the time.

monsters.intro: Corpulent humans who can change into huge swine. Love to eat human flesh. Lurk in isolated human settlements close to forests or marshes.
monsters.name: Devil Swine

abilities.name: Training
abilities.prose: At the referee’s discretion, captured cubs may be trained like dogs. Dire wolves are ferocious and extremely difficult to train.

abilities.name: Mounts
abilities.prose: Sometimes trained as mounts by goblins.

monsters.intro: Large, savage, semi-intelligent wolves. Dwell in caves, mountains, and forests.
monsters.name: Dire Wolf

abilities.name: Mundane damage immunity
abilities.prose: Can only be harmed by magical attacks.

abilities.name: Magic powers
abilities.prose: Each can be used three times per day:

abilities.name: Carrying capacity
abilities.prose: 6,000 coins without fatigue. Up to 12,000 coins for 3 turns walking / 1 turn flying. Must rest 1 turn afterwards.

abilities.name: If killed
abilities.prose: Returns to the plane of air.

monsters.intro: Highly magical, free-willed, intelligent beings from the elemental plane of air. Tall humanoids wreathed in clouds.
monsters.name: Djinni (Lesser)

abilities.name: Shape stealing
abilities.prose: Can adopt the form of any human-like creature (7’ tall or less) observed. Then proceed to attack the person mimicked.

abilities.name: Trickery
abilities.prose: Will attempt to kill a PC, take on their role, then attack the party by surprise (e.g. during a battle).

abilities.name: Reversion
abilities.prose: If killed, reverts to its original form.

abilities.name: Spell immunity
abilities.prose: Unaffected by sleep and charm spells.

monsters.intro: Intelligent, human-sized shape-shifters of wicked character and highly magical nature.
monsters.name: Doppelgänger

monsters.intro: 6’ long, carnivorous lizards with skin flaps between legs that enable gliding. Usually dwell above ground, but sometimes shelter in caves. Sometimes attack humans.
monsters.name: Draco

abilities.name: Non-combatant
abilities.prose: Will flee, if attacked.

abilities.name: Domestic
abilities.prose: Not encountered in the wild.

abilities.name: Carry loads
abilities.prose: Up to 4,500 coins unencumbered; up to 9,000 at half speed.

monsters.intro: Bred for great strength and endurance. Used to pull vehicles and ploughs or as beasts of burden.
monsters.name: Draft Horse

abilities.name: Breath weapon
abilities.prose: 90’ long, 30’ wide cloud of steam. Can be used up to three times per day. All caught in the area suffer damage equal to the dragon turtle’s current hit points (save versus breath for half).

abilities.name: Mistaken for island
abilities.prose: When floating, can be mistaken for a small island.

abilities.name: Attack ships
abilities.prose: Sometimes try to destroy ships and eat the crew by surfacing below.

abilities.name: Treasure
abilities.prose: From sunken ships.

monsters.intro: Gargantuan sea monsters with the body and hard shell of a turtle and the head, tail, and legs of a dragon. Lurk in huge caves in the depths of the ocean, only rarely coming to the surface.
monsters.name: Dragon Turtle

abilities.name: Rapacious
abilities.prose: Consume everything in their path, when hungry.

abilities.name: Morale
abilities.prose: Attack relentlessly, once they are engaged in melee (morale 12). Will even pursue through flames.

abilities.name: Lair treasure
abilities.prose: 30% chance of 1d10 × 1,000gp worth of gold nuggets, mined by the ants.

monsters.intro: Giant (6’ long), omnivorous, black ants.
monsters.name: Driver Ant

abilities.name: Bound with tree
abilities.prose: Spiritually connected with a single tree. Dryad dies if the tree dies, or if separated by more than 240’.

abilities.name: Meld with tree
abilities.prose: Can disappear by joining with her tree.

abilities.name: Defensive
abilities.prose: Distrustful of strangers. Attempt to charm anyone who approaches or follows.

abilities.name: Charm
abilities.prose: Victim compelled to approach the tree, vanishes inside it (save versus spells with a -2 penalty). If not immediately rescued, the victim is lost forever.

abilities.name: Treasure
abilities.prose: Hidden in roots of the tree.

monsters.intro: Shy, peaceful, yet wary tree spirits that can manifest as beautiful female humanoids. Live in deep forests.
monsters.name: Dryad

abilities.name: Leader
abilities.prose: A leader of level 1d6 + 2 is present for every 20 dwarves. The leader may have magic items: 5% chance per level for each magic item table (except Scrolls and Wands/Staves/Rods — see Magic Items).

abilities.name: Behaviour
abilities.prose: Chaotic dragons usually try to eat humans, but may sometimes capture them. Neutral dragons may attack or ignore humans. Lawful dragons may aid parties worthy of the honour.

abilities.name: Pride
abilities.prose: Dragons are immensely proud creatures and will always listen to flattery.

abilities.name: Attack pattern
abilities.prose: A dragon always attacks first with its breath weapon, then either breathes again or makes melee attacks (equal chance of either).

abilities.name: Energy immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by their own breath weapon or lesser versions thereof. Automatically save versus similar attack forms. (For example, a red dragon is immune to flaming oil and suffers half damage from fire ball spells.)

abilities.name: Subduing
abilities.prose: Will surrender if reduced to 0hp by non-lethal attacks (see Subduing), admitting that it has been defeated. (Subdual damage does not reduce the damage done by the breath weapon.) A subdued dragon will attempt to escape or attack its captors, if the opportunity presents itself or if given a suicidal command. A subdued dragon may be sold for up to 1,000gp per hp.

monsters.intro: Monstrous, cunning, and very intelligent hounds, the size of a small pony. Breathe fire and love heat. Dwell in dungeons or near volcanoes.
monsters.name: Hellhound (7)

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Wield giant clubs and spears.

monsters.intro: Hairy, brutish, 12’ tall humanoids of low intelligence. Dwell in foothills. Known to attack human settlements to steal food.
monsters.name: Hill Giant

abilities.name: Hate [pegasi](/srd/index.php/Pegasus "Pegasus")
abilities.prose: Will usually attack.

abilities.name: Mount
abilities.prose: Can carry a human-sized rider.

abilities.name: Taming
abilities.prose: Can be trained as mounts.

monsters.intro: Fantastic creatures combining the features of a giant eagle (head and forequarters) and a horse. Nest among craggy rocks.
monsters.name: Hippogriff

abilities.name: Hobgoblin king and bodyguards
abilities.prose: A 5HD (22hp) king and 1d4 4HD (3d6hp) bodyguards live in the hobgoblin lair. The king gains a +2 bonus to damage rolls. A thoul may sometimes be found among the king’s bodyguards.

monsters.intro: Larger and nastier relatives of goblins. Dwell underground, but commonly seek prey above ground.
monsters.name: Hobgoblin

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–5, due to camouflage.

abilities.name: Sticky tongue
abilities.prose: Can attack targets up to 5’ away. If the attack succeeds, the victim is dragged to the mouth and bitten (2d4 damage).

abilities.name: Tail
abilities.prose: Causes no damage, but knocks opponent down: cannot attack that round.

monsters.intro: 7’ long lizards whose scales change colour as camouflage.
monsters.name: Horned Chameleon

abilities.name: Heads
abilities.prose: 1d8+4 heads; 1HD per head.

abilities.name: Disabling heads
abilities.prose: For every 8hp damage taken, one head is disabled (cannot attack).

abilities.name: Variants
abilities.prose: Special hydras sometimes found with venom, fiery breath, etc.

monsters.intro: Large, dragon-like creatures with multiple, serpentine heads. Sea hydras (adapted to water, with fins) also exist.
monsters.name: Hydra (10)

abilities.name: Type and locomotion
abilities.prose: A swarm may be composed of creepers (e.g. spiders, ants, centipedes) or flyers (e.g. bees, hornets). Some swarms are capable of both types of movement (e.g. beetles, locusts).

abilities.name: Size
abilities.prose: 10’ × 30’ area, typically.

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Only harmed by fire, extreme cold, sleep spells (affect the whole swarm), smoke (drives off), or other attacks as the referee wishes.

abilities.name: Swarm attack
abilities.prose: Automatically damages characters within swarm area: 2hp if wearing armour, 4hp without.

abilities.name: Warding off
abilities.prose: Characters inside the swarm who defend themselves by brandishing a weapon (or similar) suffer half damage from the swarm. A brandished torch damages the swarm.

abilities.name: Escaping
abilities.prose: On exiting the swarm, characters continue to suffer half damage until 3 rounds are spent swatting the attached insects.

abilities.name: Diving into water
abilities.prose: Suffer damage for one round, then attached insects drown.

abilities.name: Pursuit
abilities.prose: An angry (i.e. damaged) swarm will pursue characters until they are out of sight or inaccessible.

monsters.intro: Swarms of many tiny insects that may be encountered protecting their nest. May also be drawn to light or unusual smells.
monsters.name: Insect Swarm (2)

abilities.name: Tracking
abilities.prose: Without fault.

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–5, unless target can detect invisibility.

abilities.name: If killed
abilities.prose: Returns to plane of origin.

monsters.intro: Highly intelligent, magical creatures summoned from another plane of existence to perform tasks for powerful magic-users.
monsters.name: Invisible Stalker (Monster)

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Unaffected by sleep spells.

abilities.name: Absorb metals
abilities.prose: Hits with non-magical, metal weapons cause damage, but the weapon may become stuck in the statue (save versus spells). Stuck weapons can be removed if the statue is killed.

abilities.name: Attacks
abilities.prose: Depend on form of statue (humanoid statues may use weapons, animal statues use claws, etc.).

abilities.name: Magical origins
abilities.prose: Created by powerful wizards.

monsters.intro: Animated statues of any size and material (three types described below; others may be invented by the referee).
monsters.name: Iron Living Statue

abilities.name: Aggressive
abilities.prose: Usually attack on sight. Always attack intruders within 30’ of their hive.

abilities.name: Die after attacking
abilities.prose: On a successful sting attack, a killer bee dies.

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Causes death (save vs poison).

abilities.name: Lodged stinger
abilities.prose: Inflicts 1 damage per round, as the stinger works its way in. A round can be spent to remove it.

abilities.name: Queen
abilities.prose: A 2HD queen lives in the hive. The queen does not die when she stings.

abilities.name: Guards
abilities.prose: At least 10 bees (4 or more of which have 1HD) remain in or near the hive to protect the queen.

abilities.name: Honey
abilities.prose: Magical honey (around 2 pints) may be found in the hive. It heals 1d4 hit points if eaten (in its entirety).

monsters.intro: Giant (1’ long) bees of aggressive temperament. Build hives underground.
monsters.name: Killer Bee

abilities.name: Swallow whole
abilities.prose: A natural 20 attack roll indicates a halfling-sized (or smaller) victim is swallowed. Inside the whale’s belly: suffer 1d6 damage per round (until the whale dies); may attack with sharp weapons at -4 to hit; drown after 10 rounds; body digested in 6 turns after death.

monsters.intro: 25’ long, carnivorous whales. Dwell in cold waters and hunt sea creatures (including other whales).
monsters.name: Killer Whale

abilities.name: Ambush
abilities.prose: Set up surprise attacks.

abilities.name: Hate [gnomes](/srd/index.php/Gnome "Gnome")
abilities.prose: Attack on sight.

abilities.name: Chieftain and bodyguards
abilities.prose: A 2HD (9hp) chieftain and 1d6 1+1HD (6hp) bodyguards live in the kobold lair.

abilities.name: Hoard
abilities.prose: Only have treasure type J when encountered in the wilderness or in their lair.

monsters.intro: Small, wicked, hairless, canine humanoids with scaly, rust-coloured skin. Dwell underground.
monsters.name: Kobold

abilities.name: Feeding frenzy
abilities.prose: Attracted to the scent of blood or violent movement in the water.

monsters.intro: 20’ or more long. May attack small watercraft (canoes, rafts).
monsters.name: Large Crocodile

abilities.name: Stampede
abilities.prose: Herds of 20 or more can trample those in their path. 3-in-4 chance each round. +4 to hit human-sized or smaller creatures. 1d20 damage.

abilities.name: Males
abilities.prose: In groups of 3 or more, only 1-in-4 are males. These have 1d4 extra hit points and protect the herd.

abilities.name: Females and young
abilities.prose: Flee from danger. Females do not have a butt attack. Young have half normal hit points.

monsters.intro: For example: elk or moose.
monsters.name: Large Herd Animal

abilities.name: Alignment reaction
abilities.prose: -1 to reaction rolls versus Neutral characters; -2 versus Chaotic characters.

abilities.name: Swoop
abilities.prose: Can dive onto victims visible from above. If the victim is surprised, the attack inflicts double damage. On an attack roll of 18 or more, the victim can be carried away (if of appropriate size).

abilities.name: Eggs
abilities.prose: 1d6 eggs or chicks in 50% of nests.

abilities.name: Trainable
abilities.prose: Can be trained, if captured when young.

monsters.intro: Gigantic birds of prey that nest in the highest peaks of isolated mountain ranges. Will attack intruders, if approached carelessly.
monsters.name: Large Roc

abilities.name: Blood drain
abilities.prose: Attaches to victim on a successful hit, doing 1d6 automatic damage per round.

abilities.name: Detaching
abilities.prose: Must be killed.

abilities.name: If victim dies
abilities.prose: Leech detaches and finds a hidden place to digest.

monsters.intro: Horrid, 3–4’ long, slug-like creatures with sucker mouths. Lurk in swamps.
monsters.name: Leech, Giant

abilities.name: Pursuit
abilities.prose: Always pursue prey that flees.

abilities.name: Favoured prey
abilities.prose: Often develop a taste for a certain type of meat (this may include humans!), hunting that creature with preference.

abilities.name: Inquisitive
abilities.prose: May follow PCs out of curiosity.

monsters.intro: Hunt in groups called “prides”. Live in hot regions, typically in savannah or scrublands close to deserts.
monsters.name: Lion

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Favour spears or large clubs.

abilities.name: Man-eaters
abilities.prose: Kidnap humans and demihumans, whose flesh they regard as a delicacy.

monsters.intro: Semi-intelligent, tribal, aquatic humanoids with reptilian heads and tails. Often encountered in swamps and dungeons, or along rivers and coastlines.
monsters.name: Lizard Man

abilities.name: Blood scent
abilities.prose: Can detect blood in water up to 300’ away.

abilities.name: Feeding frenzy
abilities.prose: Triggered by the scent of blood: always attack; no morale checks.

abilities.name: Unpredictable
abilities.prose: May ignore other creatures, only to attack moments later.

monsters.intro: 15’ long; bluish-grey or tan colouration.
monsters.name: Mako Shark

abilities.name: Tail spikes
abilities.prose: 180’ range. 24 in total; 2 regrow each day.

abilities.name: Track and ambush
abilities.prose: Follow humans and attack with tail spikes when they stop to rest.

monsters.intro: Monstrosities with the face of a man, the body of a lion, bat-like wings, and a tail bristling with spikes. Love eating humans. Dwell in wild, mountainous regions.
monsters.name: Manticore

abilities.name: Charge
abilities.prose: In first round of combat, when not in melee. Requires a clear run of at least 20 yards. Tusks inflict double damage.

abilities.name: Trample
abilities.prose: 3-in-4 chance of trampling each round. +4 to-hit human-sized or smaller creatures.

abilities.name: Ivory
abilities.prose: 2d4 × 100gp per tusk.

monsters.intro: Shaggy elephants with large tusks. Live in icy or Lost World regions.
monsters.name: Mastodon

abilities.name: Spells
abilities.prose: Each individual has one 1st level arcane spell memorized. (Choose or select at random.)

abilities.name: Master
abilities.prose: 50% of groups are led by a magic-user of 3rd level. The master has two 1st level spells and one 2nd level spell. (Choose or select at random.)

monsters.intro: 1st level NPC magic-users.
monsters.name: Medium

abilities.name: Stampede
abilities.prose: Herds of 20 or more can trample those in their path. 3-in-4 chance each round. +4 to hit human-sized or smaller creatures. 1d20 damage.

abilities.name: Males
abilities.prose: In groups of 3 or more, only 1-in-4 are males. These have 1d4 extra hit points and protect the herd.

abilities.name: Females and young
abilities.prose: Flee from danger. Females do not have a butt attack. Young have half normal hit points.

monsters.intro: For example: caribou or oxen.
monsters.name: Medium Herd Animal

abilities.name: Disguise
abilities.prose: Use hooded cloaks to lure victims close before revealing their true form.

abilities.name: Petrification
abilities.prose: Anyone who looks at an undisguised medusa will be turned to stone (save versus petrify).

abilities.name: Averting eyes
abilities.prose: -4 penalty to-hit; the medusa gains a +2 bonus to attack.

abilities.name: Mirrors
abilities.prose: The reflection of a medusa is harmless. If a medusa sees her own reflection, she must save or be petrified.

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: The snakes’ bites are venomous: save versus poison or die in 1 turn.

abilities.name: Magic resistance
abilities.prose: +2 bonus to saves versus spells.

monsters.intro: Deadly creatures of a magical nature that look like women with writhing snakes in place of hair.
monsters.name: Medusa

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Sword and dagger.

abilities.name: Mounted
abilities.prose: On horse, camel, or mule (by terrain).

abilities.name: Wagons
abilities.prose: 2 per merchant. Pulled by horses, mules, or camels (by terrain).

abilities.name: Caravan guards
abilities.prose: For every merchant, 4 guards are present (1st level fighters). AC 4 [15], with crossbows, swords, daggers.

abilities.name: Guard lieutenants
abilities.prose: For every 5 merchants, 2 lieutenants are present (2nd–3rd level fighters). AC 4 [15]. Equipped as guards.

abilities.name: Guard captain
abilities.prose: Guards are led by a 5th level fighter. AC 4 [15]. Equipped as guards.

abilities.name: Pack animals
abilities.prose: 1d12 extra horses, mules, or camels.

abilities.name: Treasure
abilities.prose: Should be reduced, if less than 10 merchants are in the group.

monsters.intro: Organized traders who travel between settlements in well-armed caravans, buying and selling trade goods (e.g. gold, jewels, silk, spices, wine, etc.).
monsters.name: Merchant

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Spears, tridents, or daggers.

abilities.name: Leaders
abilities.prose: For every 10 in a group, there is a 2HD leader. For every 50, there is a 4HD leader (saves: D10 W11 P12 B13 S14 (4)).

abilities.name: Underwater villages
abilities.prose: Home to 1d3 × 100 individuals.

abilities.name: Guardians
abilities.prose: Use trained sea creatures or monsters to guard their homes.

monsters.intro: Aquatic humanoids with fish tails in place of legs. Live in coastal waters, farming seaweed and hunting fish.
monsters.name: Merman

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Prefer axes, clubs, or spears.

abilities.name: Aggressive
abilities.prose: Attack same size or smaller creatures on sight. Pursue until the victims are out of sight.

monsters.intro: Large, brutish, bull-headed men with a craving for human flesh. Dwell in mazes and labyrinths.
monsters.name: Minotaur

abilities.name: Pursuit
abilities.prose: Always pursue prey that flees.

abilities.name: Favoured prey
abilities.prose: Often develop a taste for a certain type of meat (this may include humans!), hunting that creature with preference.

abilities.name: Inquisitive
abilities.prose: May follow PCs out of curiosity.

monsters.intro: Have yellow-brown fur and favour mountains, deserts, and forests. Will occasionally venture into dungeons.
monsters.name: Mountain Lion

abilities.name: Tenacious
abilities.prose: Can be taken underground, if the referee allows it.

abilities.name: Defensive
abilities.prose: May attack if threatened, but cannot be trained to attack on command.

abilities.name: Carry loads
abilities.prose: Up to 2,000 coins unencumbered; up to 4,000 at half speed.

monsters.intro: Stubborn horse/donkey cross-breeds used as beasts of burden.
monsters.name: Mule

abilities.name: Paralyse with terror
abilities.prose: Anyone seeing a mummy must save versus paralysis or be paralysed with terror. Paralysis is broken if the mummy attacks or goes out of sight.

abilities.name: Disease
abilities.prose: Anyone hit contracts a horrible rotting disease. Magical healing is ineffective; natural healing is ten times slower. The disease can only be removed by magic.

abilities.name: Damage immunity
abilities.prose: Only harmed by fire or magic. All damage reduced by half.

abilities.name: Undead
abilities.prose: Make no noise, until they attack. Immune to effects that affect living creatures (e.g. poison). Immune to mind-affecting or mind-reading spells (e.g. charm, hold, sleep).

monsters.intro: Undead humanoids wrapped in funerary bandages. Haunt ruins and tombs.
monsters.name: Mummy

abilities.name: Horn
abilities.prose: Is worth 1d6 × 1,000gp. Rumours say a narwhal’s horn vibrates when evil is near.

monsters.intro: 15’ long, intelligent, magical whales with grey to white skin and an 8’ long, spiralling horn on their nose. Live independent and secretive lives in arctic waters.
monsters.name: Narwhal

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Prefer stone axes, stone hammers, clubs, or spears.

abilities.name: Leaders
abilities.prose: Two 6HD leaders (one male, one female) live in the neanderthal lair. They are 10’ tall and of a related but distinct race.

abilities.name: Racial animosity
abilities.prose: Attack ogres on sight. Hate goblins and kobolds.

abilities.name: Pets
abilities.prose: Sometimes keep white apes as pets (see Ape, White).

monsters.intro: Squat, powerful, primitive demihumans with ape-like faces. Dwell in caverns and hunt cave bears. Shy of contact with humans (with whom they are related), but friendly with dwarves and gnomes.
monsters.name: Neanderthal (Caveman)

abilities.name: Shy
abilities.prose: Will try to charm intruders, rather than entering combat.

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Daggers and small tridents (as spears).

abilities.name: Charm
abilities.prose: 10 nixies together can cast a charm to enchant a victim to serve them for one year. Save versus spells or be charmed: move towards the nixies (resisting those who try to prevent it); defend the nixies; obey the nixies’ commands (if understood); unable to cast spells or use magic items; unable to harm the nixies. Killing the nixies breaks the charm.

abilities.name: Summon giant bass
abilities.prose: Each nixie can summon one fish to aid in combat (see Giant Bass).

abilities.name: Bestow water breathing
abilities.prose: Can cast upon charmed slaves. Lasts for one day, then must be refreshed.

monsters.intro: 3’ tall sprites that appear as attractive women with blueish, greenish, or greyish skin. Lair in the deepest parts of rivers and lakes.
monsters.name: Nixie

abilities.name: Class
abilities.prose: Usually treated as 3rd level fighters, but may be of any class and level.

abilities.name: Squire and retainers
abilities.prose: Accompanied by a 2nd level fighter (a squire) and up to ten 1st level fighters (retainers).

monsters.intro: Powerful humans with noble titles (e.g. Count, Duke, Knight, etc.). Dwell in castles.
monsters.name: Noble

abilities.name: Mounted
abilities.prose: On riding horses or (in the desert) on camels.

abilities.name: Arms (desert)
abilities.prose: 50% of group have: leather armour, shield, lance; 30% have: chainmail, shield, lance; 20% have: leather armour, shortbow.

abilities.name: Arms (steppes)
abilities.prose: 50% of group have: leather armour, shortbow; 20% have: leather armour, shield, lance; 20% have: chainmail, shortbow; 10% have: chainmail, shield, lance, and may be mounted on a war horse.

abilities.name: Leaders
abilities.prose: For every 25 nomads, there is a 2nd level fighter. For every 40 nomads, there is a 4th level fighter.

abilities.name: Camps
abilities.prose: Hunting / foraging groups commonly combine and live in a tribe of up to 300 nomad warriors.

abilities.name: Camp leaders
abilities.prose: Tribal chief (8th level fighter), plus a 5th level fighter for every 100 nomads. 50% chance of a cleric (9th level); 25% chance of a magic-user (8th level).

abilities.name: Hoard
abilities.prose: Only have treasure type A in the camp.

abilities.name: Traders
abilities.prose: Often carry trade route tales of faraway lands.

monsters.intro: Superstitious tribes who wander steppes and desert regions, living in tents or temporary huts. Demeanour depends on the tribe: some are warlike, others peaceful.
monsters.name: Nomad

abilities.name: Echolocation
abilities.prose: Unaffected by effects that impair, modify, or rely on sight. Blinded by magical silence.

abilities.name: Swarm
abilities.prose: 10 bats can swarm around a target’s head, causing confusion: -2 to attack rolls and saves; unable to cast spells.

abilities.name: Attacks
abilities.prose: As normal human.

abilities.name: Flighty
abilities.prose: Unless magically summoned or controlled, normal bats check morale every round.

monsters.intro: Nocturnal, flying mammals that roost in caves or ruins.
monsters.name: Normal Bat

abilities.name: Feeding frenzy
abilities.prose: Attracted to the scent of blood or violent movement in the water.

monsters.intro: Large reptiles that are ungainly on land and live primarily in water, lurking just beneath the surface of subtropical swamps and lazy rivers. If hungry, attack any creatures that venture into the water.
monsters.name: Normal Crocodile

abilities.name: Swoop
abilities.prose: Can dive onto victims visible from above. If the victim is surprised, the attack inflicts double damage. On an attack roll of 18 or more, the victim can be carried away (if of appropriate size).

abilities.name: Trainable
abilities.prose: Can be trained as guards or hunting animals.

monsters.intro: Small birds of prey. Will only attack humans if they appear defenceless.
monsters.name: Normal Hawk

abilities.name: Gaining XP
abilities.prose: Must select a character class after gaining XP on an adventure.

monsters.intro: Non-adventuring humans without a character class. Artists, beggars, children, craftspeople, farmers, fishermen, housewives, scholars, slaves.
monsters.name: Normal Human

abilities.name: Disease
abilities.prose: Bite has a 1-in-20 chance of infecting the target (save versus poison). The disease has a 1-in-4 chance of being deadly (die in 1d6 days). Otherwise, the victim is sick and bedridden for one month.

abilities.name: Afraid of fire
abilities.prose: Will flee fire, unless forced to fight by summoner.

abilities.name: Attacking in water
abilities.prose: May attack without penalty; excellent swimmers.

abilities.name: Pack
abilities.prose: Each group of 5–10 rats attacks as a pack. Each pack makes a single attack roll against one creature.

abilities.name: Engulf
abilities.prose: The creature attacked must save versus death or fall prone, unable to attack until able to stand up again.

monsters.intro: Swarming packs of 6” to 2’ long individuals, with brown or grey fur.
monsters.name: Normal Rat

abilities.name: Charge
abilities.prose: If disturbed or threatened, will charge in a random direction. Requires a clear run of at least 20 yards. Make a horn attack on all in the path: inflicts double damage.

abilities.name: Trample
abilities.prose: 3-in-4 chance of trampling each round. +4 to-hit human-sized or smaller creatures.

monsters.intro: Dim, armoured, herbivorous mammals.
monsters.name: Normal Rhinoceros

abilities.name: Training
abilities.prose: At the referee’s discretion, captured cubs may be trained like dogs. Wolves are difficult to train.

abilities.name: Strength in numbers
abilities.prose: Packs of 4 or more wolves have morale 8. If the pack is reduced to less than 50% of its original size, this morale bonus is lost.

monsters.intro: Dwell primarily in wild lands, but occasionally lair in caves.
monsters.name: Normal Wolf

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by all attacks except cold or fire.

abilities.name: Division
abilities.prose: Lightning or attacks with weapons cause the jelly to divide into 1d4+1 2HD jellies that do half damage.

abilities.name: Acid
abilities.prose: After a successful attack, sticks to the victim and exudes acid. The acid inflicts 2d6 damage per round to flesh and destroys cloth, leather, or wood in one round. (Stone and metal are unaffected.)

abilities.name: Seep
abilities.prose: Can squeeze through small holes and cracks.

monsters.intro: Giant, ochre amoeboids.
monsters.name: Ochre Jelly

abilities.name: Constriction
abilities.prose: Tentacles grab and constrict after a hit. Each constricting tentacle inflicts: 1d3 automatic damage per round, plus a -1 penalty to attacks.

abilities.name: Severing tentacles
abilities.prose: Requires a hit with a cutting weapon inflicting 6 or more damage.

abilities.name: Ink cloud
abilities.prose: When escaping, can emit a cloud of black ink (80’ diameter) and jet away at 3 times normal speed.

monsters.intro: Giant, eight-armed cephalopods. Lurk in coastal waters, close to settlements.
monsters.name: Octopus, Giant

abilities.name: Sack
abilities.prose: Outside of the lair, carry a sack containing 1d6 × 100gp.

abilities.name: Hate [neanderthals](/srd/index.php/Neanderthal_(Caveman) "Neanderthal (Caveman)")
abilities.prose: Attack on sight.

monsters.intro: Frightful humanoids, 8–10’ tall, dressed in animal hides. Often dwell in caves.
monsters.name: Ogre

abilities.name: Oil spray
abilities.prose: Use when attacked. Targets one opponent within 5’. A hit causes painful blistering: -2 to attack rolls for 24 hours. Cure light wounds can be used to cure this, instead of restoring hit points.

monsters.intro: 3’ long, burrowing beetles sometimes encountered below ground.
monsters.name: Oil Beetle

abilities.name: Hate the sun
abilities.prose: -1 to-hit in full daylight.

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Prefer axes, clubs, spears, or swords. Only leaders can use mechanical weapons (e.g. crossbows, catapults).

abilities.name: Craven
abilities.prose: Afraid of larger or stronger-looking creatures, though leaders may force them to fight.

abilities.name: Leader
abilities.prose: Groups are led by an orc with 8 hit points. The leader gains a +1 bonus to damage rolls. Leaders have defeated other orcs in combat to gain their position.

abilities.name: Orc chieftain
abilities.prose: A 4HD (15hp) chieftain rules an orc tribe. The chieftain gains a +2 bonus to damage rolls.

abilities.name: Giant companions
abilities.prose: For every 20 orcs, there is a 1-in-6 chance of an ogre accompanying them. There is a 1-in-10 chance of a troll living in the lair.

abilities.name: Tribal
abilities.prose: Orcs of different tribes may fight among themselves, unless their leaders command them not to. Each tribe has its own lair and has as many females as males, and two whelps per two adults.

abilities.name: Mercenaries
abilities.prose: Orcs may be hired to fight in Chaotic armies. They delight in wanton killing and razing of settlements.

monsters.intro: Ugly, bad-tempered, animalistic humanoids who live underground and are active at night. Sadistic bullies who hate other living creatures and delight in killing.
monsters.name: Orc

abilities.name: Bear hug
abilities.prose: If a victim is hit by both paws in the same round, the owl bear hugs for an extra 2d8 automatic damage.

monsters.intro: Huge (8’ tall, 1,500 pounds), ill-tempered, carnivorous bear-like creature with the face of an owl. Owl bears are found in dense forests and underground.
monsters.name: Owl Bear

abilities.name: Pursuit
abilities.prose: Always pursue prey that flees.

abilities.name: Favoured prey
abilities.prose: Often develop a taste for a certain type of meat (this may include humans!), hunting that creature with preference.

abilities.name: Inquisitive
abilities.prose: May follow PCs out of curiosity.

monsters.intro: High-speed hunters that live in plains and forests.
monsters.name: Panther

abilities.name: Hate [hippogriffs](/srd/index.php/Hippogriff "Hippogriff")
abilities.prose: Natural enemies.

abilities.name: Mount
abilities.prose: Can carry a human-sized rider.

abilities.name: Training
abilities.prose: If captured when young, will serve Lawful characters.

monsters.intro: Semi-intelligent winged horses that are wilful and timid.
monsters.name: Pegasus

abilities.name: Ships and crew
abilities.prose: Depends where encountered. Rivers or lakes: 1d8 river boats (1d2 × 10 pirates each); Coastal waters: 1d6 small galleys (1d3+1 × 10 pirates each); Any: 1d4 longships (1d3+2 × 10 pirates each); Ocean: 1d3 small warships (1d5+3 × 10 pirates each). (See Water Vessels for details on ships.)

abilities.name: Arms
abilities.prose: 50% of group have: leather armour, sword; 35% have: leather armour, sword, crossbow; 15% have: chainmail, sword.

abilities.name: Leaders
abilities.prose: For every 30 pirates, there is a 4th level fighter. For every 50 pirates, and for each ship, there is a 5th level fighter. For every 100 pirates, and per fleet, there is an 8th level fighter.

abilities.name: Fleet commander
abilities.prose: Fleets of 300 or more pirates are led by a pirate lord (11th level fighter). 75% chance of a magic-user (level 1d2+8).

abilities.name: Treacherous
abilities.prose: Will attack other pirates, if they can profit from it.

abilities.name: Prisoners
abilities.prose: 25% chance of 1d3 prisoners to be ransomed.

abilities.name: Treasure
abilities.prose: Divided between vessels. Instead of carrying with them, may have a map to where it is buried.

abilities.name: Havens
abilities.prose: Lawless, fortified, coastal towns may act as a haven for pirates.

monsters.intro: Sailors who make a living by raiding coastal settlements, robbing other ships, and illegal slaving. Typically ply the open seas. Renowned for their ruthless and evil ways.
monsters.name: Pirate

abilities.name: [Infravision](/srd/index.php/Hazards_and_Challenges#Infravision "Hazards and Challenges")
abilities.prose: 60’. (Pits in the head allow heat sense.)

abilities.name: Initiative
abilities.prose: Always gains initiative (no roll), due to special senses.

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Causes death (save vs poison).

monsters.intro: 5’ long snakes with grey/green scales.
monsters.name: Pit Viper

abilities.name: Invisible
abilities.prose: Naturally invisible, but may choose to reveal themselves. May remain invisible when attacking: cannot be attacked in the first round; in subsequent rounds, may be attacked at -2 to hit (locatable by faint shadows and air movement).

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: Always surprise, if invisible.

abilities.name: Limited flight
abilities.prose: Small wings only allow 3 turns of flight. Must rest 1 turn after flying.

monsters.intro: 1–2’ tall humanoids with insectoid wings. Distant relatives of elves.
monsters.name: Pixie

abilities.name: Bear hug
abilities.prose: If a victim is hit by both paws in the same round, the bear hugs for an extra 2d8 automatic damage.

abilities.name: Swimming
abilities.prose: Excellent swimmers.

abilities.name: Snow walk
abilities.prose: Wide paws allow running on the surface of snow without sinking.

monsters.intro: Aggressive, white-furred bears that stand 11’ tall and live in cold regions. Favours eating fish.
monsters.name: Polar Bear

monsters.intro: Wingspan of up to 50’. Highly aggressive; often prey on humanoids.
monsters.name: Pteranodon

monsters.intro: 8–10’ wingspan. Prey on small to medium animals, but will attack humanoids, if very hungry.
monsters.name: Pterodactyl

abilities.name: Swallow whole
abilities.prose: A bite attack roll of 20, or 4 or more than the target number required, indicates that a human-sized (or smaller) victim is swallowed. Inside the worm’s belly: suffer 3d6 damage per round (until the worm dies); may attack with sharp weapons at -4 to hit; body digested in 6 turns after death.

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Causes death (save vs poison).

abilities.name: In restricted spaces
abilities.prose: May not always be able to bite and sting at once.

monsters.intro: Gigantic, slimy worms with bodies 100’ long and 8–10’ thick. Bore tunnels through the earth and come to the surface to eat other creatures.
monsters.name: Purple Worm

abilities.name: Breath weapon
abilities.prose: 90’ long cone of fire.

abilities.name: [Language](/srd/index.php/Languages "Languages") and spells
abilities.prose: 50%; 3 × 1st level, 3 × 2nd level, 3 × 3rd level.

abilities.name: Behaviour
abilities.prose: Chaotic dragons usually try to eat humans, but may sometimes capture them. Neutral dragons may attack or ignore humans. Lawful dragons may aid parties worthy of the honour.

abilities.name: Pride
abilities.prose: Dragons are immensely proud creatures and will always listen to flattery.

abilities.name: Attack pattern
abilities.prose: A dragon always attacks first with its breath weapon, then either breathes again or makes melee attacks (equal chance of either).

abilities.name: Energy immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by their own breath weapon or lesser versions thereof. Automatically save versus similar attack forms. (For example, a red dragon is immune to flaming oil and suffers half damage from fire ball spells.)

abilities.name: Subduing
abilities.prose: Will surrender if reduced to 0hp by non-lethal attacks (see Subduing), admitting that it has been defeated. (Subdual damage does not reduce the damage done by the breath weapon.) A subdued dragon will attempt to escape or attack its captors, if the opportunity presents itself or if given a suicidal command. A subdued dragon may be sold for up to 1,000gp per hp.

abilities.name: Age
abilities.prose: The following stats describe dragons of average size. Younger dragons may have up to 3 HD less and ¼ or ½ as much treasure. Older dragons may have up to 3 HD more and twice as much treasure.

abilities.name: Lairs
abilities.prose: A dragon’s treasure is always kept in its well-hidden lair and is seldom unguarded.

abilities.name: Uses fire
abilities.prose: Monsters that use fire are unharmed by burning oil.

monsters.intro: Dwell in hills and mountains.
monsters.name: Red Dragon

abilities.name: Suckers
abilities.prose: Front legs equipped with suckers for grabbing prey.

abilities.name: Grab
abilities.prose: When hit with a sucker, the victim is stuck and will be bitten automatically next round.

abilities.name: Cling
abilities.prose: Can walk on walls.

monsters.intro: Huge, hairy, nocturnal arachnid carnivores with large heads and mandibles and 10 legs. Yellow head and abdomen, dark brown thorax. Dwell in caves, hunt voraciously.
monsters.name: Rhagodessa

abilities.name: Domestic
abilities.prose: Not encountered in the wild.

abilities.name: Carry loads
abilities.prose: Up to 3,000 coins unencumbered; up to 6,000 at half speed.

monsters.intro: Lightly built horses adapted to run at high speed. Can survive purely on grass, wherever available.
monsters.name: Riding Horse

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: Hunt patiently. Surprise on a 1–4, when waiting in shadows for prey.

abilities.name: Poison immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by killer bees’ poison.

abilities.name: Leap
abilities.prose: Can leap up to 30’ and attack.

monsters.intro: 3’ long, carnivorous flies with yellow and black stripes. Look similar to killer bees, which they hunt. May attack humans.
monsters.name: Robber Fly

abilities.name: Weapons
abilities.prose: Wield bones or branches as clubs. (Do not use other tools.)

monsters.intro: Large, ferocious, semi-intelligent baboons that live in packs led by a powerful male. Communicate with screams. Omnivorous, but prefer flesh.
monsters.name: Rock Baboon

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.prose: Unaffected by sleep spells.

abilities.name: Magical origins
abilities.prose: Created by powerful wizards.

monsters.intro: Stony crust filled with magma which they shoot from their fingertips.
monsters.name: Rock Living Statue

abilities.name: Constriction
abilities.prose: When a bite attack is successful, the python wraps around the victim and begins to squeeze, inflicting 2d4 automatic damage immediately and on each subsequent round.

monsters.intro: 20’ long snakes with spiralling brown and yellow patterns on their scales.
monsters.name: Rock Python

abilities.name: Rusting
abilities.prose: Metal that touches a rust monster (e.g. weapons that hit it, or armour struck by a feeler) crumbles instantly to rust. Magic items have a 10% chance per “plus”, to be unaffected on each successful hit. Each time a magic item is affected, it loses one “plus”.

abilities.name: Smell metal
abilities.prose: Attracted by the scent.

monsters.intro: Magical, armadillo-like creatures with long tails and two long, antennae-like feelers. Feed on rusted metal.
monsters.name: Rust Monster

abilities.name: Pursuit
abilities.prose: Always pursue prey that flees.

abilities.name: Favoured prey
abilities.prose: Often develop a taste for a certain type of meat (this may include humans!), hunting that creature with preference.

abilities.name: Inquisitive
abilities.prose: May follow PCs out of curiosity.

monsters.intro: Huge, aggressive cats with foot-long fangs. Normally only found in Lost World regions.
monsters.name: Sabre-Toothed Tiger

abilities.name: Irritant spray
abilities.prose: Above water; maximum once a turn. Can spray one target: save versus poison or stunned for 1 turn.

abilities.name: Ink cloud
abilities.prose: Underwater; maximum once a turn. When escaping, can emit a cloud of black ink to confuse attackers.

abilities.name: Eat ships
abilities.prose: Cling to bottom of vessel. Each individual inflicts 1d3 hull damage then drops off.

abilities.name: Noticing ship damage
abilities.prose: 50% chance per round of spotting leaks.

monsters.intro: Giant (1’–5’ long), wood-eating, aquatic insects with a sack to take in and jet out water. Prone to feeding on passing ships. Only attack creatures if cornered.
monsters.name: Saltwater Termite

abilities.name: Aggressive
abilities.prose: Normally attack on sight.

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Causes death (save vs poison).

abilities.name: Grab and sting
abilities.prose: +2 bonus to sting attack, if a claw hits.

monsters.intro: Huge arachnids, as big as a small horse, with pincers and deadly stingers. Dwell in caverns, ruins, and deserts.
monsters.name: Scorpion, Giant

abilities.name: Breath weapon
abilities.prose: Poison spittle, 100’ range, 20’ diameter. Save versus breath or die. (Poison is harmless after 1 round.)

abilities.name: [Language](/srd/index.php/Languages "Languages") and spells
abilities.prose: 20%; 3 × 1st level, 3 × 2nd level.

abilities.name: Gliding
abilities.prose: Leap out of the water and glide for up to 6 rounds.

monsters.intro: Intelligent, aquatic dragons with green scales, fin-like wings, and a yellowish crest. Dwell in the depths of the ocean, inhabiting caverns or shipwrecks. Sometimes attack vessels to steal their treasure and eat the crew.

abilities.name: Lunge
abilities.prose: Up to 20’ out of water to use bite attack.

abilities.name: Squeeze
abilities.prose: Coil around and crush a vessel (of equal size or smaller).

monsters.intro: 20’–30’ long, serpent-like sea monsters with rows of many fins.
monsters.name: Sea Serpent (Lesser)

abilities.name: Pinprick bite
abilities.prose: 50% chance of going unnoticed.

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Slow acting: effects felt after 1d4+2 turns. Save vs poison or die 1 turn later. At this point, the neutralize poison spell has a 25% chance of not working.

abilities.name: Larger individuals
abilities.prose: Sea snakes with more than 3 HD may be encountered. They are 6’ long for every 3 HD.

monsters.intro: 6’ long snakes that live underwater, coming up for breath only once per hour. Will prey on humans.
monsters.name: Sea Snake

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–5.

abilities.name: Strength drain
abilities.prose: Victims lose 1 STR per hit. Recovers after 8 turns. If reduced to 0 STR, the victim becomes a shadow.

abilities.name: Mundane damage immunity
abilities.prose: Can only be harmed by magical attacks.

abilities.name: Spell immunity
abilities.prose: Unaffected by charm and sleep spells.

monsters.intro: Intelligent, incorporeal (but not undead) monsters that look like shadows. Able to slightly change their shape.
monsters.name: Shadow

abilities.name: Initiative
abilities.prose: Always win initiative in the round of their first attack. +1 to initiative in the round of their second attack.

abilities.name: Ferocity
abilities.prose: Attack targets’ heads. Targets with 3 HD or less must save versus death or flee.

abilities.name: Climbing
abilities.prose: Skilled climbers; can jump up to 5’.

abilities.name: Territorial
abilities.prose: Ferociously defend their hunting area from all intruders.

abilities.name: Echolocation
abilities.prose: Perceive their surroundings up to 60’. Unaffected by lack of light. If unable to hear (e.g. silence, 15’ radius): AC reduced to 8 [11], -4 penalty to attacks.

monsters.intro: Brown-furred, mole-like, insectivores with long snouts. Dwell underground; skilled burrowers.
monsters.name: Shrew, Giant

abilities.name: Shriek
abilities.prose: Triggered by light (up to 60’ away) or movement (up to 30’ away). Shriek lasts for 1d3 rounds. Each round of the shriek, there is a 50% chance of a wandering monster being attracted (will arrive in 2d6 rounds).

monsters.intro: Giant, subterranean mushrooms that can creep around slowly.
monsters.name: Shrieker

abilities.name: Undead
abilities.prose: Make no noise, until they attack. Immune to effects that affect living creatures (e.g. poison). Immune to mind-affecting or mind-reading spells (e.g. charm, hold, sleep).

monsters.intro: Skeletal remains of humanoids, reanimated as guardians by powerful magic-users or clerics. Often encountered in cemeteries, crypts, or other forlorn places.
monsters.name: Skeleton

abilities.name: Stampede
abilities.prose: Herds of 20 or more can trample those in their path. 3-in-4 chance each round. +4 to hit human-sized or smaller creatures. 1d20 damage.

abilities.name: Males
abilities.prose: In groups of 3 or more, only 1-in-4 are males. These have 1d4 extra hit points and protect the herd.

abilities.name: Females and young
abilities.prose: Flee from danger. Females do not have a butt attack. Young have half normal hit points.

monsters.intro: For example: antelope, deer, goats.
monsters.name: Small Herd Animal (1)

abilities.name: Alignment reaction
abilities.prose: -1 to reaction rolls versus Neutral characters; -2 versus Chaotic characters.

abilities.name: Swoop
abilities.prose: Can dive onto victims visible from above. If the victim is surprised, the attack inflicts double damage. On an attack roll of 18 or more, the victim can be carried away (if of appropriate size).

abilities.name: Eggs
abilities.prose: 1d6 eggs or chicks in 50% of nests.

abilities.name: Trainable
abilities.prose: Can be trained, if captured when young.

monsters.intro: Gigantic birds of prey that nest in the highest peaks of isolated mountain ranges. Will attack intruders, if approached carelessly.
monsters.name: Small Roc

abilities.prose: A successfully hit target permanently loses two experience levels (or Hit Dice). This incurs a loss of two Hit Dice of hit points, as well as all other benefits due to the drained levels (e.g. spells, saving throws, etc.). A character’s XP is reduced to the lowest amount for the new level. A person drained of all levels becomes a spectre next night, under the control of the spectre that killed them.

monsters.intro: Incorporeal phantoms; one of the most powerful undead monsters.
monsters.name: Spectre

abilities.name: Ram ships
abilities.prose: 10% likely to attack vessels

monsters.intro: Gargantuan whales up to 60’ long. Dwell in open oceans and hunt deep sea monsters (e.g. giant squids).
monsters.name: Sperm Whale

abilities.name: Blinding spit
abilities.prose: Range: 6’. A hit causes permanent blindness (save vs poison).

abilities.name: Poison
abilities.prose: Causes death in 1d10 turns (save versus poison).

monsters.intro: 3’ long snakes with grey/white scales. Prefer to attack from a distance with spit.
monsters.name: Spitting Cobra

abilities.name: Curse
abilities.prose: Five sprites can collectively curse a target (no attack roll; save versus spells). The effect is determined by the referee, but will always be used to comical effect (e.g. target trips, target’s nose grows).

abilities.name: Pranksters
abilities.prose: Even if attacked, sprites have no interest in killing, merely in making practical jokes.

monsters.intro: 1’ tall, winged humanoids, related to pixies and elves. They are shy, but driven by curiosity and their odd sense of humour.
monsters.name: Sprite

abilities.name: Grab crew
abilities.prose: 75% likely to grab crew from the deck and drag them underwater to be eaten.

abilities.name: Constriction

abilities.name: Gargantuan specimens
abilities.prose: Two or three times larger can be encountered.

monsters.intro: Giant, ten-armed cephalopods. Two of the ten arms are larger, and may be used to attack ships. Dwell in the depths of the sea, only surface to find prey.
