abilities.name: Regeneration
abilities.prose: A damaged thoul gains 1 hit point at the start of each round, as long as it is alive.

monsters.intro: Magical monstrosities that look like hobgoblins (except on close inspection), but combine the powers of ghouls and trolls. Sometimes live among hobgoblins.
monsters.name: Thoul

abilities.name: Pursuit
abilities.prose: Always pursue prey that flees.

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–4, in woodland, due to camouflage.

abilities.name: Favoured prey
abilities.prose: Often develop a taste for a certain type of meat (this may include humans!), hunting that creature with preference.

abilities.name: Inquisitive
abilities.prose: May follow PCs out of curiosity.

monsters.intro: Large, solitary hunters with striped camouflage markings. Favour woodlands and cooler regions.
monsters.name: Tiger

monsters.intro: Carnivorous, 4’ long, tiger-striped beetles with powerful, crushing mandibles. Hunt robber flies, but sometimes eat humans.
monsters.name: Tiger Beetle

abilities.name: Peaceful grazers
abilities.prose: Will not attack if left alone.

abilities.name: Trample
abilities.prose: 3-in-4 chance of trampling each round. +4 to-hit human-sized or smaller creatures.

monsters.intro: Giant (12’ at the shoulder), ancient relatives of the rhinoceros, with massive, blunt horns. Eat grass and tree leaves. Dwell in small herds in Lost World grasslands.
monsters.name: Titanothere

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–3, in forests or dark dungeons, due to the ability to change colour to match their surrounding.

abilities.name: Sticky tongue
abilities.prose: Attack up to 15’ away. On a hit, prey (up to dwarf size) is dragged to the mouth and bitten.

abilities.name: Swallow whole
abilities.prose: A natural 20 attack roll indicates a small victim is swallowed. Inside the toad’s belly: suffer 1d6 damage per round (until the toad dies); may attack with sharp weapons at -4 to hit; body digested in 6 turns after death.

monsters.intro: Warty amphibians with long, sticky tongues. As large as a great hound, weighing 150 to 250 pounds.
monsters.name: Toad, Giant

abilities.name: Arms
abilities.prose: Usually carry: hand-axe, sword, shield, furs (equivalent to leather armour).

abilities.name: Mules
abilities.prose: In the wilderness, have 1d4 mules loaded with trade goods (e.g. carved wooden items, furs, spices).

monsters.intro: 1st level fighters who live by trading in borderland areas.
monsters.name: Trader

abilities.name: Distrust fire
abilities.prose: And those who wield it.

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–3, in a forest, due to being mistaken for a tree. Encounter occurs at 30 yards or less.

abilities.name: Animate trees
abilities.prose: Each individual can animate 2 trees (within 60’; may switch trees at will). These fight as treants with movement rate 30’ (10’).

monsters.intro: Giant (18’ tall) humanoids that look like trees. Dwell in forests. Are concerned only with protecting the plants of their home. Speak their own long-winded and circuitous language.
monsters.name: Treant

abilities.name: Aggressive
abilities.prose: Normally attack on sight.

abilities.name: Charge
abilities.prose: In first round of combat, when not in melee. Requires a clear run of at least 20 yards. Horns inflict double damage.

abilities.name: Trample
abilities.prose: 3-in-4 chance of trampling each round. +4 to-hit human-sized or smaller creatures.

monsters.intro: Massive (12’ to the shoulder, almost 40’ long), aggressive, herbivorous dinosaurs with a protective crest behind the head, and three long horns. Dwell in Lost World grasslands.
monsters.name: Triceratops

abilities.name: Hateful
abilities.prose: Attempt to kill any creatures they encounter.

abilities.name: Surprise
abilities.prose: On a 1–4, due to the ability to change colour to match their surroundings. Lurk by rock walls and await victims.

abilities.name: Nauseating stench
abilities.prose: Oils on the skin have a smell that sickens humans and demihumans: save versus poison or suffer -2 to hit, while in melee with troglodytes.

monsters.intro: Intelligent, reptilian humanoids with agile hands, long legs, short tails, and spiky combs on their heads and arms.
monsters.name: Troglodyte

abilities.name: Regeneration
abilities.prose: 3 rounds after being damaged, start regaining 3hp per round. Severed limbs reattach.

abilities.name: Return from death
abilities.prose: If killed (0hp), will regenerate and fight again in 2d6 rounds.

abilities.name: Fire and acid
abilities.prose: Cannot regenerate damage from these sources. The only way to permanently kill a troll.

abilities.name: Fear of fire
abilities.prose: Morale 8 when attacked with fire or acid.

monsters.intro: Intelligent, wicked, 8’ tall humanoids with emaciated, rubbery bodies. Consume the flesh of other humanoids. Dwell underground, in barren wilderness, and in the ruined homes of former victims.
monsters.name: Troll

abilities.name: [Infravision](/srd/index.php/Hazards_and_Challenges#Infravision "Hazards and Challenges")
abilities.prose: 90’. Granted by retractable eye membranes.

monsters.intro: 8’ long, iguana-like, carnivorous lizards with olive scales and a ridge of white spikes along the back. Have been known to attack humans.
monsters.name: Tuatara

abilities.name: Large prey
abilities.prose: Attack largest target first.

monsters.intro: Great (over 20’ tall), two-legged, predatory dinosaurs with huge jaws. Hunt human-sized or larger prey. Dwell in Lost World regions.
monsters.name: Tyrannosaurus Rex

abilities.name: Empathy with maidens
abilities.prose: A pure-hearted maiden can communicate with and ride a unicorn.

abilities.name: Teleport
abilities.prose: Once per day, up to 360’ (including rider).

monsters.intro: Fantastic creatures that appear as elegant horses with a long horn. Unicorns are timid, but proud and wilful.
monsters.name: Unicorn

abilities.name: Undead
abilities.prose: Make no noise, until they attack. Immune to effects that affect living creatures (e.g. poison). Immune to mind-affecting or mind-reading spells (e.g. charm, hold, sleep).

abilities.name: Mundane damage immunity
abilities.prose: Can only be harmed by magical attacks.

abilities.name: Energy drain
abilities.prose: A successfully touched target permanently loses two experience levels (or Hit Dice). This incurs a loss of two Hit Dice of hit points, as well as all other benefits due to the drained levels (e.g. spells, saving throws, etc.). A character’s XP is reduced to the lowest amount for the new level. A person drained of all levels becomes a vampire in 3 days.

abilities.name: Charming gaze
abilities.prose: Save versus spells at -2 or be charmed: move towards the vampire (resisting those who try to prevent it); defend the vampire; obey the vampire’s commands (if understood); unable to cast spells or use magic items; unable to harm the vampire. Killing the vampire breaks the charm.

abilities.name: Regeneration
abilities.prose: A damaged vampire gains 3hp at the start of each round, as long as it is alive.

abilities.name: At 0hp
abilities.prose: Change into gaseous form; flee to coffin.

abilities.name: Change form
abilities.prose: At will; takes 1 round:

abilities.name: Summon beasts
abilities.prose: In human form only. Creatures from the surrounding area: 1d10 × 10 rats, 5d4 giant rats, 1d10 × 10 bats, 3d6 giant bats, 3d6 wolves, or 2d4 dire wolves.

abilities.name: Coffins
abilities.prose: Must rest in a coffin during the day or lose 2d6hp (only regenerated by resting a full day). Cannot rest in a blessed coffin. Always keep multiple coffins in hidden locations.

monsters.intro: Greatly feared undead monsters that live by drinking the blood of mortals. Dwell in ruins, tombs, and deserted locales.

abilities.name: Behaviour
abilities.prose: Chaotic dragons usually try to eat humans, but may sometimes capture them. Neutral dragons may attack or ignore humans. Lawful dragons may aid parties worthy of the honour.

abilities.name: Pride
abilities.prose: Dragons are immensely proud creatures and will always listen to flattery.

abilities.name: Attack pattern
abilities.prose: A dragon always attacks first with its breath weapon, then either breathes again or makes melee attacks (equal chance of either).

abilities.name: Energy immunity
abilities.prose: Unharmed by their own breath weapon or lesser versions thereof. Automatically save versus similar attack forms. (For example, a red dragon is immune to flaming oil and suffers half damage from fire ball spells.)

abilities.name: Subduing
abilities.prose: Will surrender if reduced to 0hp by non-lethal attacks (see Subduing), admitting that it has been defeated. (Subdual damage does not reduce the damage done by the breath weapon.) A subdued dragon will attempt to escape or attack its captors, if the opportunity presents itself or if given a suicidal command. A subdued dragon may be sold for up to 1,000gp per hp.

abilities.name: Age
abilities.prose: The following stats describe dragons of average size. Younger dragons may have up to 3 HD less and ¼ or ½ as much treasure. Older dragons may have up to 3 HD more and twice as much treasure.

abilities.name: Lairs
abilities.prose: A dragon’s treasure is always kept in its well-hidden lair and is seldom unguarded.

monsters.intro: Found in cold regions.
monsters.name: White Dragon

abilities.name: Erosion
abilities.prose: Wood or leather in contact with the mould will be eaten away.

monsters.intro: Deadly fungus that covers walls, ceilings, and other surfaces.
monsters.name: Yellow Mould

abilities.name: Guardians
abilities.prose: Always attack on sight.

abilities.name: Initiative
abilities.prose: Always lose (no roll).

abilities.name: Undead
abilities.prose: Make no noise, until they attack. Immune to effects that affect living creatures (e.g. poison). Immune to mind-affecting or mind-reading spells (e.g. charm, hold, sleep).

monsters.intro: Listless, humanoid corpses, reanimated as guardians by powerful clerics or wizards.
monsters.name: Zombie
